currencyNote: Prices are approximate one-way add-on prices in USD where the airline publishes a stable starting fee or common baseline. Use null where fees are too route-dependent or require a calculator.

airlines.name: American Airlines
airlines.checkedBagFeeNote: AA publishes different checked-bag fees by region and ticketed date; domestic/short-haul often $45–$50 (online vs airport) for bag 1 and $55–$60 for bag 2.
airlines.notes: Basic/Main usually pay for checked bags. Personal item max 45x35x20 cm.

airlines.name: Delta Air Lines
airlines.checkedBagFeeNote: Delta publishes domestic baseline checked-bag fees ($45/$55) but notes fees vary by route, fare class, and ticket purchase date.
airlines.notes: Carry-on + personal item free even on Basic. Personal item size not formally listed.

airlines.name: United Airlines
airlines.checkedBagFeeNote: United encourages using its baggage fee calculator; fees vary by route/fare/status. Prepaying online can be cheaper than paying at the airport.
airlines.notes: Basic Economy generally gets only a personal item; carry-on starts at $45 if prepaid.

airlines.name: Ryanair
airlines.checkedBagFeeNote: Ryanair’s 10kg/20kg check-in bag prices vary by route and travel dates; buying at the airport or gate is usually higher.
airlines.notes: Strict sizer enforcement. Personal bag 40x30x20 cm; Priority adds 10kg cabin bag. 10kg and 20kg checked options sold separately.

airlines.name: easyJet
airlines.checkedBagFeeNote: easyJet hold-luggage prices vary per flight and are cheaper online; adding bags at the airport is significantly more expensive.
airlines.notes: Everyone gets one under-seat bag. Large cabin bag is usually paid unless bundled. Hold-bag options start at 15kg; 23kg is standard.

airlines.name: Lufthansa
airlines.checkedBagFeeNote: Lufthansa’s checked-bag fees depend on route and fare (often via baggage calculator), especially for Economy Light.
airlines.notes: Economy Light usually excludes checked bags. Optional checked-bag pricing is route-dependent via baggage calculator.

airlines.name: Air France
airlines.notes: Economy cabin baggage is 0-1 hand bag depending on fare plus 1 small bag; 12kg combined cabin weight. Light fares can exclude cabin bag on some channels.

airlines.name: Emirates
airlines.notes: Economy uses weight concept on most routes: Special 20kg, Saver 25kg, Flex 30kg, Flex Plus 35kg. Cabin bag is one 55x38x22 cm piece up to 7kg.

airlines.name: Singapore Airlines
airlines.notes: Economy allows 1 cabin bag up to 7kg and 115cm total dimensions. Checked allowance is usually 25kg (Lite/Value) or 30kg (Standard/Flexi) outside the USA.

airlines.name: ANA
airlines.notes: Most travellers get 1 carry-on + 1 personal item; total cabin weight 10kg. Economy Light/Value routes often drop from 2 free checked bags to 1.

airlines.name: Southwest Airlines
airlines.notes: Two checked bags usually included. Simple baggage rules.

airlines.name: JetBlue
airlines.checkedBagFeeNote: JetBlue checked-bag fees vary by route, peak/off-peak dates, and when you add the bag (early vs within 24h).
airlines.notes: Blue Basic usually excludes carry-on except some long-haul/international cases.

airlines.name: Alaska Airlines
airlines.notes: Personal item + carry-on usually included for all fares.

airlines.name: Air Canada
airlines.checkedBagFeeNote: Air Canada publishes checked-bag fees that vary by fare brand and itinerary (e.g., for North America/sun routes ticketed on/after Apr 13, 2026: bag 1 $45 on Basic/Standard; bag 2 $60; Flex often includes bag 1).
airlines.notes: Basic Economy usually excludes standard carry-on on North America routes. Cabin weight 10kg.

airlines.name: British Airways
airlines.checkedBagFeeNote: British Airways sells checked bags on Economy Basic; price varies by route and when you prepay (use the baggage calculator/Manage My Booking).
airlines.notes: Generous cabin allowance. Checked-bag pricing varies a lot by route.

airlines.name: KLM
airlines.notes: Economy Light can exclude cabin bag on some channels. Cabin weight usually combined.

airlines.name: Vueling
airlines.checkedBagFeeNote: Vueling publishes wide checked-bag price ranges by destination and purchase channel (web/app vs airport).
airlines.notes: Strict budget-carrier enforcement. Paid cabin bag or bundle on many fares.

airlines.name: Wizz Air
airlines.notes: Very strict sizer culture. Hold bag tiers commonly 10/20/26/32kg.

airlines.name: Turkish Airlines
airlines.notes: Allowance and fees vary by route and piece vs weight concept.

airlines.name: Qatar Airways
airlines.notes: Many routes use weight concept. Cabin bag limit usually 7kg in Economy.

airlines.name: Etihad Airways
airlines.notes: Allowance varies by route and branded fare. Extra-bag fees often route-based.

airlines.name: Cathay Pacific
airlines.notes: Most routes use piece concept. Light fare often excludes checked bag.

airlines.name: EVA Air
airlines.notes: Carry-on plus small personal item. Fees vary by route.

airlines.name: Korean Air
airlines.notes: Typical long-haul piece allowances. Extra-bag pricing varies by route.

airlines.name: Japan Airlines
airlines.notes: Generous carry-on weight. Piece and route rules vary by market.

airlines.name: China Eastern
airlines.notes: Carry-on rules are fairly standard. Extra-bag fees often route-based.

airlines.name: China Southern
airlines.notes: Long-haul piece concept common; detailed fees vary by route.

airlines.name: AirAsia
airlines.notes: Cabin bags usually 7kg total with one small item. Hold bags are add-ons in 15/20/25/30/40kg tiers.

airlines.name: IndiGo
airlines.notes: Indian domestic baseline often 15kg checked and 7kg cabin. Extra baggage is prepaid in slabs.

airlines.name: Virgin Atlantic
airlines.notes: Generous cabin weight. Extra baggage pricing depends on route.

airlines.name: Frontier Airlines
airlines.notes: Ultra low-cost. Personal item only on cheapest fare.

airlines.name: Spirit Airlines
airlines.notes: Budget carrier. Cheapest fare excludes carry-on.

airlines.name: Hawaiian Airlines
airlines.notes: Standard US baggage rules.

airlines.name: El Al
airlines.notes: Pricing varies heavily by route.

airlines.name: LOT Polish Airlines
airlines.notes: Typical European legacy carrier.

airlines.name: SWISS
airlines.notes: Similar to Lufthansa.

airlines.name: TAP Air Portugal
airlines.notes: Discount fares often exclude standard cabin bag.

airlines.name: Scandinavian Airlines
airlines.notes: Go Light can exclude cabin bag depending on market.

airlines.name: Iberia
airlines.notes: Standard EU legacy.

airlines.name: ITA Airways
airlines.notes: Successor to Alitalia. Route-dependent fees.

airlines.name: Pegasus Airlines
airlines.notes: Low-cost carrier. Strict cabin enforcement.

airlines.name: Saudia
airlines.notes: Route-dependent baggage concepts.

airlines.name: Oman Air
airlines.notes: Weight concept common on many routes.

airlines.name: Malaysia Airlines
airlines.notes: Fairly standard Asia legacy carrier allowances.

airlines.name: Scoot
airlines.notes: Low-cost long-haul. Paid checked bags in Economy.

airlines.name: Jetstar
airlines.checkedBagFeeNote: Jetstar checked-baggage pricing varies by route and purchase channel; airport pricing for the first 20kg is published (and is higher than online).
airlines.notes: Budget airline. Extra bag pricing varies.

airlines.name: Qantas
airlines.notes: Australia domestic and international rules can differ slightly.

airlines.name: Air New Zealand
airlines.notes: Domestic and long-haul fare bundles differ somewhat.
